rationale: The cited paper is not a passing reference; it is one of the focal commentaries that this response article explicitly addresses. Avnet and Higgins directly discuss Schwarz (2006)'s proposed feelings-as-information interpretation of fit effects and evaluate it against their own two-component account. They acknowledge it as a possible partial explanation but argue that it is insufficient without the strengthened-engagement mechanism, making the citation function a meaningful interpretive dispute.
relation_description: The citing paper directly responds to Schwarz (2006) by contesting the claim that regulatory fit effects can be explained mainly through feelings-as-information inference. It argues that Schwarz's account is only part of the story and that increased engagement strength is a separate, critical mechanism.
